content.full_text:
l

California
Fair Political
Practices
Commission

SUPERSEDED
by § 89503(a)

April 18, 1995

Helen H. Suhr
Baker & McKenzie
Two Embarcadero Center,
Twenty-Fourth Floor
San Francisco, CA 94111-3909

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Assistance
Our File No. I-95-098

Dear Ms. Suhr:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance
provided to you concerning the conflict of interest disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Since your letter is a
general inquiry, we consider your letter to be a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

You asked if the $280 gift limit and prohibition on the
receipt of honoraria apply to board members of public retirement
systems who are either appointed to their positions or are elected
by the membership of the retirement system.

As we discussed, if retirement board members are deemed to be
public officials who manage public investments as that term is
used in Government Code Section 87200, these board members report
their economic interests on Form 721 statement of economic
interests. In this capacity, board members are subject to the
disclosure and disqualification provisions of the Act. Officials
whose positions are included in Government Code Section 87200 are
not required to be included in a conflict of interest code for a
local government agency. Additionally, retirement board members
who are elected by the membership of the retirement system are not
deemed to be local elected officials. Therefore, these retirement
board members are not currently subject to the provisions of the

_______________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Ms. Suhr
Our File No. I-95-098
Page Two

gift limits and honoraria ban found in Section 89501 since they
are not local elected officials or designated employees in a
conflict of interest code. As you are aware, legislation has been
proposed to impose the gift limits and honoraria ban on such
officials in the future.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[illegible signature]
by: Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: Do the $280 gift limit and the prohibition on the receipt of honoraria apply to board members of public retirement systems who are either appointed to their positions or are elected by the membership of the retirement system?
sections.conclusion: If retirement board members are deemed to be public officials who manage public investments as defined in Government Code Section 87200, they report on Form 721 and are subject to disclosure and disqualification provisions. However, retirement board members elected by the membership are not deemed local elected officials and are therefore not currently subject to the gift limits and honoraria ban in Section 89501, unless they are designated employees in a conflict of interest code.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether retirement board members appointed or elected by system membership are subject to the $280 gift limit and honoraria prohibition under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Retirement board members managing public investments under Section 87200 must file Form 721 disclosures and follow disqualification rules, but elected retirement board members are not subject to gift limits and honoraria restrictions as they are not considered local elected officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document marked 'SUPERSEDED' at top; informal advice letter (Regulation 18329(c)); no formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers but clear Q/A structure in body text; responsive letter confirms prior telephone discussion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the $280 gift limit and the prohibition on the receipt of honoraria apply to board members of public retirement systems who are either appointed to their positions or are elected by the membership of the retirement system?

CONCLUSION: If retirement board members are deemed to be public officials who manage public investments as defined in Government Code Section 87200, they report on Form 721 and are subject to disclosure and disqualification provisions. However, retirement board members elected by the membership are not deemed local elected officials and are therefore not currently subject to the gift limits and honoraria ban in Section 89501, unless they are designated employees in a conflict of interest code.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter confirming telephone assistance regarding whether $280 gift limits and honoraria prohibitions apply to public retirement system board members. The response clarifies that while such board members may be subject to economic disclosure requirements if they manage public investments, elected retirement board members are not currently subject to gift and honoraria restrictions.
